creator: vvd@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Dan Mahoney from comment #3)
1.
>> - fix warnings from portclippy,
> Straightforward, I'll do that in my own time (i.e. not on this issue).
> I think I last tested this with only portlint (and poudriere testport).
I can create separate commits: 1) add dns/public_suffix_list only, 2) "improve port" with all other changes.

2.
>> - adjust whitespaces,
> Could you clarify?
Align values in LICENSE* variables, remove/add several empty lines.

3.
>> - remove GNU_CONFIGURE_MANPREFIX,
> I didn't know that was a thing, but sure, can do that (again, outside of scope for this issue)
This was temporarily added during the man migration from "${PREFIX}/man" to "${PREFIX}/share/man".
Since the migration has long been completed and the default value of this variable is the same "${PREFIX}/share", it must be deleted, which is what we do when we update a port.

4.
>> - use "${DISTVERSION:S/./-/g}" in GH_TAGNAME instead of hardcoded version "1-4-2":
> Can also do that!
If you want, we can make all the additional changes in a separate commit.
